Reading the numbers — every metric in plain English
Allocations leads handed to a banker — the at-bats Credit Pulls leads who agreed to a credit review — first real commitment Apps full applications taken — the deal is on paper Folders loan files opened — the unit everything is scored in (one ≈ $176K, field median) Volume the dollars inside those folders Closings folders that funded — money actually moved Conversion of every 100 leads allocated, how many became folders Same Day Service (SDS) share of folders opened the same day the lead arrived — pure speed Pull Through of every 100 folders opened, how many survived to closing Avg Size dollars per folder — the size of the swing Folders / Banker team output with headcount removed — the fair fight Grade weighted percentile vs all ranked bankers — A means top of the field Pace to Goal production ÷ leadership's goal — 100% means the promise was kept

Big Board

Full Rankings · All Qualified Bankers

Click any column to sort · Click a name to pull the file · Hover any header for its meaning
Methodology. Window: May 1 – Jun 30, 2026. Field: all bankers across 8 SVP sites (Garcia, Hindy, Wargo, Rice, Gooden, Spaulding, Bogojevski, Barba). Qualification minimum for rankings: 20 folders. All category ranks are field-wide across every site — the site filter narrows the view, not the competition. Trend section compares weeks 1–4 (May 4–29) vs weeks 5–8 (Jun 1–26) from Mon–Fri weekly exports. Count metrics use per-week averages; Conversion, SDS, and Folders/Banker use pooled half totals (volume-weighted), not averages of weekly percentages. Banker trend rankings require 20+ total folders AND 5+ folders in each half — this excludes new-hire ramp-ups and departures whose near-zero baselines produce meaningless percentage swings (their trends remain visible in their player files). Week 4 (May 25–29) contains Memorial Day; first-half baselines are slightly deflated league-wide, so relative movement between sites matters more than absolute trend magnitude. Overall grade is a weighted percentile composite — Folders 25%, Folder Volume 20%, Overall Conversion (Allocate → Folder) 20%, Same Day Service rate 15%, Pull Through (Folder → Close) 10%, Avg Folder Size 10%. Category ranks are against the full qualified pool. Impact projections model the weakest funnel stage moving to pool median at current upstream activity, valued at the banker's own average folder size. Same Day Service rate = Same Day Service ÷ Folders. Pull Through = Closings ÷ Folders (window-based; tail loans still in process understate late-window closers). Director War Room ranks each DMB team against all 64 DMB teams league-wide (minimum 5 bankers and 100 folders); team push lists and incentive projections use the same median-lift model as player files, halved to a per-month figure.
